questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider the graph of the function $y=e^{\ln \left(\frac{x+3}{x+1}\right)}$ then which of the following is correct
options: Graph lies completely above the x -axis
questions.solution:
$\begin{gathered}y=\frac{x+3}{x+1}\gt0 \Rightarrow x \lt -3 \text { or } x\gt-1 \\ x \rightarrow-3 \quad x \rightarrow \infty\end{gathered}$
or $y \rightarrow 0 \quad y \rightarrow 1$
$(0,1) \cup(1, \infty)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let a function $f(x), x \neq 0$ be such that $f(x)+f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=f(x) \cdot f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)$ then $f(x)$ can be :
questions.solution:
A \& B are obvious
$\begin{aligned} & \left(\mathrm{f}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)-1\right)=\frac{1}{(\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})-1)} \text { i.e., }\left(\mathrm{f}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)-1\right) \text { isreciprocal of }(\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})-1) \\ & \text { Now, forf } \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{\pi / 2}{\tan ^{-1}|\mathrm{x}|}, \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})-1=\frac{\cot ^{-1}|\mathrm{x}|}{\tan ^{-1}|\mathrm{x}|}, \mathrm{f}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)-1=\frac{\tan ^{-1}|\mathrm{x}|}{\cot ^{-1}|\mathrm{x}|} \\ & \text { Alsofor } \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{2}{1+\mathrm{k} \ell \mathrm{n}|\mathrm{x}|}, \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})-1=\frac{1-\mathrm{k} \ell \mathrm{n}|\mathrm{x}|}{1+\mathrm{k} \ell \mathrm{n}|\mathrm{x}|} \mathrm{f}\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)-1=\frac{1+\mathrm{k} \ell \mathrm{n}|\mathrm{x}|}{1-\mathrm{k} \ell \mathrm{n}|\mathrm{x}|}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following function is periodic?
options: $f(x)=\operatorname{sgn}\left(e^{-x}\right)$ where $\operatorname{sgn} x$ denotes signum function of $x$.
$f(x)=\left[x+\frac{1}{2}\right]+\left[x-\frac{1}{2}\right]+2[-x]$ where $[x]$ denotes greatest integer less than or equal to $x$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { (A) } f(x)=\operatorname{sgn}\left(e^{-x}\right)=1 \forall x \in R \\
& \Rightarrow f(x) \text { is periodic. }
\end{aligned}$ (As constant function is periodic function.)
(B) $f(x)=\sin x+|\sin x|$.
Clearly $f(x)$ is periodic with fundamental period which is L.C.M. of $2 \pi$ and $\pi$ i.e. $2 \pi$.
(C) As $\sin x \leq|x| \forall x \in R$
Clearly $f(x)=\sin x$
Hence $f(x)=\min .(\sin x|x|)=\sin x$
$\Rightarrow f(x)$ is periodic.
(D) $f(x)=\left(x+\frac{1}{2}\right)-\left\{x+\frac{1}{2}\right\}+\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)-\left\{x-\frac{1}{2}\right\}+2((-x)-\{x\})$
$\therefore d f(x)=-\left\{x+\frac{1}{2}\right\}-\left\{x-\frac{1}{2}\right\}-2\{x\}$
Clearly $f(x)$ is periodic with fundamental period 1
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be a monic polynomial of degree 5 . The graph of $|f(x)|$ and $f(|x|)$ are same. If $f(2)=0$. Then:
options: $\int\left(\frac{x}{f(x)}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} d x=\ln \left(x+\sqrt{x^2-4}\right)+C$ where $C$ is constant of integration
questions.solution:
If graph of $|f(x)|$ and $f(|x|)$ is same then $f(x)>0 \forall x>0$
Given $f(2)=0 \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(2)=0$ because $f(x)$ cannot be -ve for $x>0$. 
By symmetry $f(-2)=f^{\prime}(-2)=0$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=x(x-2)^2(x+2)^2$ (Note: Function must be odd)
$f(x)=x\left(x^2-4\right)^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=\frac{\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^6-\left(x^6+\frac{1}{x^6}\right)-2}{\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^3+\left(x^3+\frac{1}{x^3}\right)}$ then
questions.solution: If we simplify, we get $f(x)=3\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f_1(x)=x, f_2(x)=1-x, f_3(x)=\frac{1}{x}, f_4(x)=\frac{1}{1-x}, f_5(x)=\frac{x}{x-1}, f_6(x)=\frac{x-1}{x}$ suppose that $f_6\left(f_m(x)\right)=f_4(x)$ and $f_n\left(f_4(x)\right)=f_3(x)$ then
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & f_6\left(f_m(x)\right)=f_4(x)=\frac{1}{1-x} \\ & f_6\left(f_m(x)\right)=\frac{f_m(x)-1}{f_m(x)}=\frac{1}{1-x}\end{aligned}$

Let $f_m(x)=k \Rightarrow f_m(x)=\frac{x-1}{x}=f_6(x) \Rightarrow m=6$
Assign, $f_n\left(f_4(x)\right)=f_3(x)=\frac{1}{x}$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{n}}\left(\frac{1}{1-\mathrm{x}}\right)=\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}} \Rightarrow$ let $\frac{1}{1-\mathrm{x}}=\mathrm{t} \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=\frac{\mathrm{t}-1}{\mathrm{t}}$ hence, $\mathrm{n}=5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(g(x))$ is a one-one function, $f: R \rightarrow R$ and $g: R \rightarrow R$, then
questions.solution:
Since $f(g(x))$ is a one-one function $\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}\left(\mathrm{g}\left(\mathrm{x}_1\right)\right) \neq \mathrm{f}\left(\mathrm{g}\left(\mathrm{x}_2\right)\right)$ whenever $\mathrm{x}_1 \neq \mathrm{x}_2$
$\Rightarrow g\left(x_1\right) \neq g\left(x_2\right)$ whenever $x_1 \neq x_2$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})$ must be one-one.
Let $f(x)=y$ is satisfied by $x=x_1$ and $x_2$
If $g(x)$ is such that its range has only one of $x_1$ and $x_2$, then $f(g(x))$ can be one-one even if $f(x)$ is many-one
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f:(0, \infty) \rightarrow[-2, \infty), f(x)=a x^2-b x+c$ (where $\left.a, b, c \in R\right)$ be a surjective function such that $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} f(x)=3$. If $g:[1, \infty) \rightarrow[-2, \infty), g(x)=f(x)$ is an invertible function, then identify which of the statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: The value of $40 \cdot g^{\prime}(1)$ is equal to 0 .
If domain of $g(g(x))$ is $\left[1+\sqrt{\frac{p}{q}}, \infty\right)$, then $(q-p)$ equal to 2 .
The number of solution(s) of the equation $g(x)=g^{-1}(x)$ is 2 .
The value of $\frac{d}{d x}\left(90 g^{-1}(x)\right)$ at $x=43$ is 3 .
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} f(x) & =5 x^2-10 x+3=5(x-1)^2-2=g(x) \\ f(x) & =a(x-1)^2-2 \\ \lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{+}} f(x) & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{+}}\left[a(x-1)^2-2\right]=3\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow & a-2=3 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a=5 \\ \therefore & g:[1, \infty) \rightarrow[-2, \infty) \\ & g(x)=5(x-1)^2-2\end{aligned}$ (1) $g^{\prime}(x)=10(x-1) \quad \Rightarrow g^{\prime}(1)=0$ (2) Domain of $g(g(x))$ $\begin{aligned} & g(x) \geq 1 \Rightarrow 5(x-1)^2-2 \geq 1 \\ & x \geq 1+\sqrt{\frac{3}{5}} \\ & \therefore \quad x \in\left[1+\sqrt{\frac{3}{5}}, \infty\right) \equiv\left[1+\sqrt{\frac{p}{q}}, \infty\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad q-p=2\end{aligned}$ (3) $\begin{aligned} & g(x)=g^{-1}(x)=x \\ & 5\left(x^2-2 x+1\right)-2=x \quad \Rightarrow \quad 5 x^2-11 x+3=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{11 \pm \sqrt{121-60}}{10}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { (4) }\left.\frac{d}{d x}\left[90\left(g^{-1}(x)\right)\right]\right|_{x=43}=\frac{90}{g^{\prime}(4)}=\frac{90}{10(3)}=3 \\ & g(x)=43 \Rightarrow 5(x-1)^2-2=43 \Rightarrow x-1=3 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x=4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider, $f(f(x-2))=\left(x^2+3\right)^2+1 \forall x \in R$.
Identify which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: If $\int \frac{d x}{f(x)}=g(x)+C$ where $C$ is a constant and $g(-2)=0$, then $g(0)+g(1)=\frac{3 \pi}{4}$
questions.solution:
$f(f(x-2))=\left(x^2+3\right)^2+1$
$x-2 \rightarrow t$
$\begin{array}{ll} & f(f(t))=\left((t+2)^2+3\right)^2+1=\left(t^2+4 t+7\right)^2+1=\left(t^2+4 t+5+2\right)^2+1 \\ & f(f(t))=\left(t^2+4 t+5\right)^2+4\left(t^2+4 t+5\right)+5 \\ \therefore \quad & f(x)=x^2+4 x+5\end{array}$
(1) $f(x)=(x+2)^2+1 \Rightarrow$ Least value of $f(x)$ is 1 .
(3) $\frac{d(f(f(x)))}{d x}=2\left((t+2)^2+3\right) \cdot(2(t+2))$
$\left.\frac{d(f(f(x)))}{d x}\right|_{x=0}=2 \cdot 7 \cdot 4=56$
(4) $\int \frac{d x}{(x+2)^2+1}=\tan ^{-1}(x+2)+C$
$\therefore \quad g(x)=\tan ^{-1}(x+2) \Rightarrow g(0)+g(1)=\tan ^{-1} 2+\tan ^{-1} 3=\frac{3 \pi}{4}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f$ be a real valued function defined on $R$ (the set of all real numbers) as $f(x)=\pi\left\{\frac{x}{\pi}\right\}$, then which of the following is(are) correct?
[Note: where $\{\cdot\}$ denotes fractional part function.]
questions.solution: Graph of $y=f(x)$ Clearly, range of $f(x)$ is $[0, \pi)$ $\int_0^{2 \pi} f(x) d x=2 \times \frac{1}{2} \times \pi \times \pi=\pi^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=(a x+b) \cos x+(c x+d) \sin x$ and $f^{\prime}(x)=x \cos x$ for all $x \in R$, then
questions.solution: We have $a \cos x-(a x+b) \sin x+c \sin x+(c x+d) \cos x=x \cos x \forall x \in R$ $\Rightarrow(a+d) \cos x+(c-b) \sin x-a x \sin x+(c-1) x \cos x=0$ is an identity $\Rightarrow \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{d}=0, \mathrm{c}-\mathrm{b}=0, \mathrm{a}=0, \mathrm{c}-1=0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=\mathrm{d}=0$ and $\mathrm{b}=\mathrm{c}=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following function(s) is(are) surjective?
options:
$k: D_k \rightarrow R^{+}, k(x)=\sqrt{[x]+[-x]+1}+\sqrt{\{x\}+\{-x\}+1}$
[Note: $[m]$ and $\{m\}$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $m$ and fraction part function of $m$ respectively, and $D_l$ denotes the domain of the funct1ion $y=l(x)$.]
questions.solution:
() $\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\ln \left(\tan \pi[x]+\left|x^2+2 x-3\right|\right) \\ & \therefore \quad[x] \in I \quad \Rightarrow \quad \tan \pi[x]=0, \\ & \text { and } \quad\left|x^2+2 x-3\right|=\left|(x+1)^2-2\right| \in[0, \infty)\end{aligned}$
So, $f(x) \in R \quad \Rightarrow \quad f(x)$ is surjective.
(2) $\begin{aligned} & g(x)=\frac{x^2+2 x-3}{x-1}, x \neq 1 \\ & g(x)=\frac{(x-1)(x+3)}{(x-1)}, x \neq 1 \\ & g(x)=x+3 \quad \therefore \quad g(x) \neq 4 \quad(\because x \neq 1)\end{aligned}$
So, range of $g(x)$ is $R-\{4\}$.
$\Rightarrow \quad g(x)$ is not surjective.
(3) $\quad h(x)=\ln \left(\frac{1-x}{1+x}\right), \quad \frac{1-x}{1+x}>0$
$\because \quad \frac{1-x}{1+x}$ take all value between $(0, \infty)$
So, range of $h(x)=R$
$\Rightarrow \quad h(x)$ is surjective.
(4) $k(x) \sqrt{[x]+[-x]+1}+\sqrt{\{x\}+\{-x\}+1}$
Domain of $k(x)$ is $R$
$\begin{aligned} & x \notin I \quad \Rightarrow \quad \text { and } \quad\{x\}+[-x]=-1 \quad-x\}=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad k(x)=\sqrt{2} \\ & x \in I \quad \Rightarrow \quad[x]+[-x]=0 \quad \text { and } \quad\{x\}+\{-x\}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad k(x)=2 \\ & \end{aligned}$
So, range of $k(x)=\{\sqrt{2}, 2\}$
So, $k(x)$ is not surjective.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f$ be real-valued function such that $e^{-2 x} f(x)=x+3+\int_0^x \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t^6+1}}$ for all $x \in(-1,1)$ and let $y=g(x)$ be a function whose graph is reflection of the graph of $y=f(x)$ w.r.t. line $y=x$, then $g^{\prime}(3)$ is not equal to:
questions.solution:
Clearly $g(x)=f^{-1}(x)$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad g^{\prime}(f(x))=\frac{1}{f^{\prime}(x)} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad g^{\prime}(3)=\frac{1}{f^{\prime}(0)}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & f(x)=e^{2 x}+2(x+3) e^{2 x}+2 e^{2 x} \int_0^x \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t^6+1}}+e^{2 x} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{x^6+1}}\right) \\ \therefore & f^{\prime}(0)=1+6+0+1=8 \\ \therefore & g^{\prime}(3)=\frac{1}{8}\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)=x^2-p x+q, p, q \in R$ such that $f(x)=f(6-x) \forall x \in R$ and least value of $f(x)$ is $\frac{-81}{4}$ then:
options: largest integral value of $k$ for which equation $\operatorname{sgn}(f(x)+k)=0$ has a solution is 20 .

largest integral value of $k$ for which equation $\operatorname{sgn}(f(x)+k)=0$ has a solution is 21 .
[Note: $[y]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $y$ and $\operatorname{sgn}(y)$ denotes the signum function of $y$.]
questions.solution:
Least value of $f(x)$ is $\frac{-D}{4 a}=\frac{-81}{4}$ Least value of $\tan ^{-1}(22+[f(x)])$
$\begin{aligned} & \tan ^{-1}\left(22+\left[-\frac{81}{4}\right]\right) \\ & \tan ^{-1}(22-21)=\frac{\pi}{4}\end{aligned}$
Largest integral value of $k$ for which equation
$\operatorname{sgn}(f(x)+k)=0$ has a solution
$\because \quad f(x)$ has min. value -20.25
If $k>20.25$, then $\operatorname{sgn}(f(x)+k)$ is always positive.
So, largest integral value of $k$ is 20 .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)$ is a monic polynomial function of degree 4 satisfying $f(i)=\frac{1}{i}$ for $i=1,2,3,4$ then:
options: number of zeroes at the end of $f(5)$ ! is 4 .
sum of even divisors of $f(5)$ is 56 .
sum of odd divisors of $f(5)$ is 18 .
questions.solution:
$x f(x)-1 \equiv(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)(x-4)(x-\alpha)$
Put $x=0 \Rightarrow \alpha=\frac{1}{24}$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=\frac{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)(x-4)\left(x-\frac{1}{24}\right)+1}{x}$
$\Rightarrow \quad f(5)=\frac{4 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1 \times \frac{119}{24}+1}{5}=24$
Now, verify the options.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ is non-negative function defined for $x \geq 1$ such that $f^{\prime}(x) \leq m f(x)$ holds everywhere in the domain for some positive real number $m$. If $f(1)=0$ then:
options: Number of solutions of the equation $f(x)=e^x-x^2$ is 1 .
questions.solution:
$f(x)=0 \forall x \geq 1$
(a) is true because domain of $f(x)$ is $x \geq 1$.
(c) is false because domain of $f(x)$ is $x \geq 1$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If $f: A \rightarrow B, f(x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{[x]}{\{x\}}\right)$ and $g: C \rightarrow D, g(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{[x]}{\{x\}}\right)$, then which of the following is always correct?
[Note: [ $\cdot$ ] and $\{\cdot\}$ denotes greatest integer and fractional part function respectively.]
options: Number of integral solution of the equation $f(x)+g(x)=\frac{\pi}{2}$ is zero.
questions.solution:
Clearly, $\quad f(x)$ and $g(x)$ is defined if $-1 \leq \frac{[x]}{\{x\}} \leq 1$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad 0 \leq\{x\} < 1 \text { and }[x] \in I \\ & \therefore \quad 0 < x < 1 \Rightarrow \frac{[x]}{\{x\}}=0\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad A=C=(0,1)$ and $f(x)=0$ and $g(x)=\frac{\pi}{2} \forall x \in(0,1)$
$B$ and $D$ are co-domain
$\therefore \quad$ Need not to be singleton sets.
$f(x)+g(x)=\frac{\pi}{2} \forall x \in(0,1)$
$\therefore \quad$ No. of integral solution $=0$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let : $f:[0, \infty] \rightarrow A ; f(x)=\sqrt{\tan ^{-1} x}+\sqrt{\pi-\tan ^{-1} x}$ is an onto function, then:
questions.solution:
$\tan ^{-1} x=t \in\left(\frac{-\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$
and $f(x)$ is defined when $0 \leq t < \frac{\pi}{2}$, where $f^{\prime}(x)>0$ so injective.
Also, at $\quad t=0 \rightarrow f(x)=\sqrt{0}+\sqrt{\pi}=\sqrt{\pi}$
at $\quad t=\frac{\pi}{2} \rightarrow f(x)=\sqrt{\frac{\pi}{2}}+\sqrt{\frac{\pi}{2}}=2 \sqrt{\frac{\pi}{2}}=\sqrt{2 \pi}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider, $f(x)=3\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{x-2}\right)^2-\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} \sqrt{x}$. Identify which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
questions.solution:
$f(x)=3\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{x-2}\right)^2+\sec ^{-1} \sqrt{x}-\frac{\pi}{2}$
$\therefore \quad$ Range $=\left[\frac{-\pi}{4}, \frac{3 \pi^2}{4}\right)$
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 2^{+}} \frac{3\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{x-2}\right)^2-\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} \sqrt{x}+\frac{\pi}{4}}{\sin (x-2)}=\lim _{t \rightarrow 0^{+}} \frac{3\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{t}\right)^2-\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} \sqrt{t+2}-\frac{\pi}{4}}{t}$
$=\lim _{t \rightarrow 0^{+}}\left(\frac{3\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{t}\right)^2}{(\sqrt{t})^2}-\frac{\cot ^{-1}(\sqrt{t+1})-\tan ^{-1}(1)}{t}\right)=\frac{1}{4}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Which of the following functions are continuous $\forall x>1$ ?
[Note: Where $[k]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$ and $\{k\}$ denotes fractional part function of $k$.]
questions.solution:
Check at $x=k \quad k \in I$
(a) $\left.\quad \begin{array}{l}f\left(k^{+}\right)=k \\ f\left(k^{-}\right)=k-1+1=k\end{array}\right]$ continuous
(b) $\begin{aligned} f\left(k^{+}\right) & =k^2+0+2 k \cdot 0=k^2 \\ f\left(k^{-}\right) & =(k-1)^2+1+2+2(k-1)(1)=k^2\end{aligned}$
(c) for $x>1 \quad\{x\}$ is less than $e^x$ and both function are positive.
$\therefore \quad \forall x>1 \quad 0 < \frac{\{x\}}{e^x} < 1$
$\therefore \quad[]=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad$ continuous
(d) $\begin{aligned}& f\left(k^{+}\right)=0 \\& f\left(k^{-}\right)=[] \times 0=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \text { continuous }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $y=f(x)$ be function defined as $x=y^3+y^2+y+1$, then which of the following is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Let $g(x)=f^{-1}(x) \Rightarrow g(x)=x^3+x^2+x+1$
$f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{g^{\prime}(f(x))} \quad$ and $\quad f^{\prime \prime}(x)=\frac{-g^{\prime \prime}(f(x))}{\left(g^{\prime}(f(x))\right)^3}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & f^{\prime}(0)=\frac{1}{g^{\prime}(f(0))}=\frac{1}{g^{\prime}(-1)}=\frac{1}{2} \\ \Rightarrow & f^{\prime \prime}(0)=\frac{-g^{\prime \prime}(-1)}{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^3}=32\end{array}$
$I=\int_0^4 f(x) d x$
put $\quad x=g(t)$
$I=\int_{-1}^1 t\left(3 t^2+2 t+1\right) d t=\frac{4}{3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The value of 'a' for which the function $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}-x^3+\cos ^{-1} a, & 0 \lt x \lt 1 \\ x^2, & x \geq 1\end{array}\right.$ has a local minimum at $x=1$,is -
questions.solution:
For $\mathrm{x} \geq 1 f(\mathrm{x})$ is an increasing function. For minimum at $x=1$, so
$\begin{aligned} & f\left(1^{-}\right) \geq f(1) \quad \Rightarrow \quad-1+\cos ^{-1} \mathrm{a} \geq 1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{a} \leq \cos 2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f: R \rightarrow[-1,1]$ be defined as $f(x)=\cos (\sin x)$ then which of the following is/are correct?
options: $f$ is neither even nor odd function
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& (A)f(x)=\cos (\sin x) \\
& \text { As } f(x+\pi)=\cos (\sin (\pi+x))=\cos (-\sin x)=\cos (\sin x)=f(x) \\
& \text (B) { As }-1 \leq \sin x \leq 1 \\
& \cos 1 \leq \cos (\sin x) \leq 1 \\
& (C)\lim _{x \rightarrow \pi / 2}\left[f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)+f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}+x\right)\right] \\
& =\lim _{x \rightarrow \pi / 2} 2 \cos (\cos x)=2
\end{aligned}$
(D) Clearly $f(x)$ is even function
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f$ be a quadratic polynomial such that $f(-1-x)=f(-1+x), \forall x \in R$.
If $(f(1)-5)^2+(f(-1)-1)^2=f^{\prime}(-1)$, then which of the following is(are) equal to unity?
(where $[\cdot]$ denotes greatest integer function.)
questions.solution:
$\because \quad f(-1-x)=f(-1+x)$
$\therefore \quad f(x)$ is symmetrical w.r.t. line $x=-1$.
$\therefore \quad f(x)=a(x+1)^2+b$
$f^{\prime}(-1)=0$
$\therefore \quad f(1)=5$ and $f(-1)=1$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad b=1, \quad a=1 \\ & \therefore \quad f(x)=(x+1)^2+1=x^2+2 x+2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
